new_classes.label_en: Distillation process from grape wine or grape marc
new_classes.definition_en: A distillation process in which the input material is grape wine or grape marc (pomace), as specified for CN subheading 2208 20. Includes primary distillation after alcoholic fermentation of grape wine to produce wine distillate (raw distillate of wine).

new_classes.label_en: Grape wine or grape marc origin quality
new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by a spirit indicating that it was produced from grape wine or grape marc (pomace) as the source material, discriminating CN subheading 2208 20 from other spirits under heading 2208.

new_classes.label_en: Spirits obtained by distilling grape wine or grape marc (CN 2208 20)
new_classes.definition_en: Spirituous beverage classified under CN subheading 2208 20, produced by distillation of grape wine or grape marc (pomace), with an alcoholic strength of less than 80% vol, undenatured. Includes wine distillate (raw distillate of wine) obtained by primary distillation after alcoholic fermentation of grape wine. Does not have the properties of neutral ethyl alcohol or a finished spirit in the strict sense.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the four main discriminating criteria from the legal text: (1) the product is a spirit obtained by distillation (SpiritousDistillationProcess subtype); (2) the source material is grape wine or grape marc (GrapeWineOrMarcOriginQuality); (3) it is undenatured ethyl alcohol with alcoholic strength less than 80% vol (UndenaturedEthylAlcoholLessThan80VolQuality + decimalRange restriction); (4) it belongs to the parent heading 2208 class. The legal note also clarifies that wine distillate (raw distillate of wine) from primary distillation is included and that the product does NOT have the properties of neutral ethyl alcohol or a finished spirit — this negative characteristic (not neutral spirit, not fully rectified) cannot be fully expressed in OWL without a complement restriction on a 'NeutralEthylAlcohol' class that does not yet exist in the TBox, hence a small coverage gap (~0.12). The note reference to HS EN heading 2208, third paragraph (1) is purely administrative and carries no additional product-discriminating content beyond what is already encoded.
